channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Rising concern at 'risky' rural reliance on foreign satellite technology
item.description: Phone, internet and council-run TV services have been making way for foreign satellite technology in rural Queensland as ground infrastructure ages, forcing councils to shut down services.
item.dc:creator: Jay Carstens

media:group.media:description: Robert Stubbs, like many in the outback, has made the switch to Starlink to work around the dying signal in Jericho.

item.title: Ian Walker's death haunted his friends. His sister's love solved the mystery
item.description: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.
item.dc:creator: Russell Jackson

media:group.media:description: Karen Walker (left) has searched alone for answers about the crimes that ruined her brother's life and hastened his death.

item.title: King opens UK parliament amid palace concerns over leadership chaos
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister remains on life support amid mounting speculation he could face a leadership challenge as early as Thursday, local time.
item.dc:creator: Riley Stuart and Mazoe Ford

media:group.media:description: Sir Keir Starmer is fighting for his political survival.

item.title: 'Not legally possible' to explain: Secrecy surrounds Tasmanian MP's legal bills
item.description: A large sum of public money has been spent on legal fees for politicians. The Tasmanian government says in one case, it's "not legally possible" to explain why.
item.dc:creator: Lucy MacDonald

media:group.media:description: Integrity Commission rules prevent the person under investigation from being identified.

item.title: 'Buried' report shows Australians waiting 12 months for aged care support
item.description: Opposition MPs accuse the federal government of burying figures showing Australians are waiting a year for residential aged care and at-home support packages.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Dalzell and Paige Cockburn

media:group.media:description: The federal government released the data while journalists were in budget lock-up on Tuesday.

item.title: Accommodation provider fined for guide dog refusal in Qld first case
item.description: A Queensland accommodation provider has been the first to be successful convicted under the state's Guide, Hearing and Assistance Dogs (GHAD) Act.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Richards

media:group.media:description: Janice Whittle and her guide dog Keegan were denied accommodation by a provider in Agnes Water.

item.title: Angus Taylor proposes one-for-one migration cap tied to housing
item.description: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but would wait until in government to decide what visa types to slash if elected.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor wants to limit Australia's migration intake.

item.title: In Trump-Xi China meeting only one leader has the upper hand
item.description: Nine years after his last meeting with Xi Jinping in China, Donald Trump is more aggressive and assertive. But so is Beijing. And its president is ready to take the geopolitical and economic lead.
item.dc:creator: Allyson Horn

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump arrived in Beijing last night for the first visit to China by a US president since 2017.

item.title: Rider crashes, takes wrong turn, still wins wild Giro d'Italia stage
item.description: The Giro d'Italia treated supporters to one of the most astonishing finishes to a bike race in history when Igor Arrieta had to twice regather himself after first crashing and later taking a wrong turn to claim a stunning victory.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Astonishingly, Igor Arrieta claimed victory.

item.title: The rising populist tide is making Labor bolder
item.description: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.
item.dc:creator: David Speers

media:group.media:description: Labor sees this populist wave as a threat and is going for policy boldness in response.

item.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul
item.description: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.
item.dc:creator: Jasper Wells, Michael Janda, and Stephanie Chalmers

media:group.media:description: Matthew Bowes says home ownership will likely increase, as homebuyers will be able to outbid investors at auctions.

item.title: Blockbuster movies are connecting new audiences to classical music
item.description: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.
item.dc:creator: Ria Andriani

media:group.media:description: How to Train Your Dragon has become one of the most iconic and most widely attended films‑in‑concert.

item.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition
item.description: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

media:group.media:description: Participant Kate Miller-Heidke of Australia performs during the Grand Final of the 2019 Eurovision Song Contest in Tel Aviv, Israel May 18, 2019.

item.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel will bewitch you in Mother Mary
item.description: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.
item.dc:creator: Stephen A Russell

media:group.media:description: Anne Hathaway transforms into a world-renowned pop star in Mother Mary

item.title: Oldest evidence of dentistry found in 59,000-year-old Neanderthal tooth
item.description: Archaeologists have found a hole in a 59,000-year-old tooth, which they say was drilled to treat a painful cavity. The find suggests Neanderthals could perform complex medical procedures.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Phiddian

media:group.media:description: A Neanderthal had a tooth drilled to treat a painful cavity some 59,000 years ago, according to a new study.

item.title: Kate tours Italy in first overseas visit since cancer recovery
item.description: The royal is visiting Reggio Emilia, the Italian city famed for its approach to early childhood education.

media:group.media:description: The Princess of Wales is visiting the Italian city of Reggio Emilia, which is notable for its innovative approach to early childhood education.

item.title: Six dead as Russia launches over 800 drones at Ukraine
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy accuses Russia of attempting to overload Ukraine's defences in an hours-long onslaught.

media:group.media:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says six people are dead after one of Russia's biggest drone attacks of the war so far.

item.title: At least 12 people killed in Israeli strikes on cars in Lebanon
item.description: The deadly drone strikes come on the eve of a new round of peace talks between Israel and Lebanon in the United States.

media:group.media:description: An Israeli airstrike hit a car in the Lebanese coastal town of Jiyeh, south of Beirut.

item.title: UK MP Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
item.description: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift that he did not declare.

media:group.media:description: Britain's Reform UK office is communicating with the parliamentary commissioner for standards.

item.title: Donald Trump arrives in Beijing ahead of Xi Jinping summit
item.description: The ​US and China are expected to agree to forums to facilitate mutual trade and investment.

media:group.media:description: US President Donald Trump has arrived in Beijing.

item.title: Shots fired in Philippine senate as ICC seeks to arrest ex-police chief
item.description: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.
item.dc:creator: Claire Campbell

media:group.media:description: Military personnel enter the senate building where Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa has been under protective custody since Monday.

item.title: Australian government secures aircraft to fly home Hantavirus cruise passengers
item.description: A suitable aircraft to bring home passengers from the Hantavirus-hit cruise has been secured and will repatriate the group of six later this week.
item.dc:creator: Anna Cox

media:group.media:description: Mark Butler earlier revealed the struggle to secure a suitable aircraft for the mission.

item.title: 'Portrait of anger' by Australian cartoonist wins $60,000 Stella Prize
item.description: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.
item.dc:creator: Nicola Heath

media:group.media:description: Lee Lai, an Australian cartoonist based in Montreal, has won the 2026 Stella Prize for her graphic novel Cannon.

item.title: Is the 2026 budget a missed opportunity for First Nations people?
item.description: First Nations experts have their say on whether the $1.2 billion for Closing the Gap initiatives announced in this year's budget is likely to hit the mark.
item.dc:creator: Tahnee Jash and Stephanie Boltje

media:group.media:description: Indigenous people have double the rate of unmet housing needs.

item.title: NT government accused of 'politicising tragedy' with child protection laws
item.description: Peak Aboriginal bodies have accused the Northern Territory government of using Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged murder as political cover to push ahead with sweeping changes to child protection laws.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Breen

media:group.media:description: Ben Grimes says the "rushed" law changes represent "the politicisation of a tragedy".
